results.title: Take-Two Interactive AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Take-Two Interactive has taken a cautious stance on AI implementation in game development, with CEO Strauss Zelnick emphasizing that while AI can enhance efficiency, it cannot replace human creativity[1][2]. The company uses AI tools for non-creative tasks like mundane operational work, but explicitly states it is not using AI as an excuse to reduce headcount[3]. Zelnick argues that AI is inherently 'backward-looking' and data-driven, making it unsuitable for the forward-looking creativity required for franchises like Grand Theft Auto[4]. Despite this skeptical view of AI's creative capabilities, Take-Two acknowledges that AI will become a useful tool when 'properly deployed' to help streamline workflows and free up creative talent for more interesting tasks[5].
...

...
Take-Two shows minimal AI adoption in hiring with only 5.4% of job postings mentioning AI. Notably, tech roles show 0% AI mentions while non-tech roles show 7%, suggesting AI requirements are emerging in business functions rather than technical development roles. Marketing leads at 18.2%, aligning with the company's cautious stance on AI for operational rather than creative uses.

...
### Enterprise AI Tools for Operational Efficiency
...
Implementation of AI tools in non-creative business areas to automate mundane tasks and improve operational efficiency
...
Take-Two is rolling out AI tools across the enterprise to help with routine tasks, explicitly not for reducing headcount but to free up talent for more creative work. The company reports seeing 'good, early results' in creating efficiencies.
...
### AI-Enhanced Character Interactions for Future Games
...
Exploring AI training for game characters to enable more natural conversations based on human-written scripts
...
Zelnick envisions game characters being AI-trained on scripts created by great writers to interact more naturally with players, moving beyond traditional scripted interactions. This would allow characters to respond dynamically while maintaining the quality of human-created content.
...
Does Take-Two plan to use AI to replace human developers?
...
No, Take-Two explicitly states they are not using AI as an excuse to reduce headcount. Instead, they aim to use AI to free up talented people from mundane tasks so they can focus on more creative and interesting work.
...
AI create games
...
No, CEO Strauss Zelnick believes AI cannot create games like Grand Theft Auto because AI is inherently 'backward-looking' and data-driven, while creativity is 'forward-looking.' He states that AI would produce something 'pretty derivative' rather than innovative.
...
Take-Two's CEO argues there is 'no creativity that can exist by definition in any AI model' because it is data-driven. He views AI as a useful tool for efficiency but not for replacing human creativity in game development.
...
Take-Two envisions using AI to train game characters on scripts written by human writers, allowing for more natural and dynamic interactions with players while maintaining the quality of human-created content.
...
What areas of Take-Two's business currently use AI?
...
Take-Two uses AI tools in non-creative areas of the business, particularly for operational efficiency and automating mundane tasks. They are seeing early positive results in creating efficiencies across the enterprise.

results.title: Shashanka Prajapati
highlights:
Data Scientist at Take-Two Interactive
...
Data Scientist at [Take-Two Interactive](https://www.linkedin.com/company/take-2-interactive-software-inc-)
...
I love coding and when it comes to coding solutions to problems involving logical thinking, I would gladly burn the midnight oil any day. I taught myself Python during the early years of my Bachelor's degree. My earliest efforts into Natural Language Processing were the Sentiment Analysis and Voice Assistant projects. This knowledge helped me get an internship where I developed a Bilingual Search Engine (looking back, an unpaid intern for five months makes me want to cry). Following that, I made the best professional decision of my life. I decided to apply for the AI Fellowship program at Fusemachines, which helped me get a job there. I officially entered the world of Artificial Intelligence, which provided me with a lot of new possibilities. At Fusemachines, I worked as a developer and team lead on a range of projects, including Search Optimization, Computer Vision, PDF Parsing, Recommendation Systems, Time-Series Forecasting, and more. In addition, I've had the opportunity to learn Agile Development and MLOps, as well as engage in research on Python Project Standardization, Airflow Automation, and Front-End Development. I'm always interested in learning new things and will take advantage of any opportunity to improve my knowledge and skills. When I'm not learning or coding, I like to watch anime, series, and movies, as well as play mobile games. I also enjoy reading manga and light novels from time to time. ...
# Take-Two Interactive is hiring a IP Enforcement Analyst
...
Headquartered in New York City, Take-Two Interactive Software, Inc. is a leading developer, publisher, and marketer of interactive entertainment for consumers around the globe. We develop and publish products principally through Rockstar Games, 2K, and Zynga. Our strategy is to create hit entertainment experiences, delivered on every platform relevant to our audience through a variety of sound business models. Our pillars - creativity, innovation, and efficiency - guide us as we strive to create the highest quality, most captivating experiences for our consumers. The Company’s common stock is publicly traded on NASDAQ under the symbol TTWO. For more corporate and product information please visit our website at http://www.take2games.com.
...
The Take-Two Interactive Legal Publishing team is responsible for protecting genre-defining franchises including Grand Theft Auto, Borderlands, Bioshock, Civilization, and more. We disrupt harmful and unauthorized commercial exploitation of world-famous IP while ensuring that our fans and creator communities can engage with the properties that they love.
...
As IP Enforcement Analyst you combine an investigative mindset and familiarity with the video game industry to execute our IP protection strategy, safeguarding fan communities while aggressively targeting commercialized IP theft. Reporting to legal counsel based in Singapore, you are responsible for the identification, triage, and removal of infringing content across a variety of social media and ecommerce platforms. You will collaborate with team members located in India, the US, and key support staff at our outside IP enforcement vendors.
...
- Manage and analyze daily intake of infringing leads to distinguish between legitimate community engagement and actionable copyright or trademark infringement.
...
- Draft and process DMCA takedown notices and trademark infringement reports across all major social media platforms, ecommerce marketplaces, and common gamer-community platforms (Discord, Telegram, Reddit, modding platforms).
...
- Perform rigorous
...
level infringement investigations, compiling evidence and identifying bad actors to support legal enforcement action.
...
- Conduct proactive research to identify emerging threats and grey-market trends.
...
- Serve as a primary interface for our outside brand
...
- University degree in law, business, or information technology.
...
- 2-4 years experience in Brand Protection, IP Enforcement, Anti-Fraud / Digital Risk Management or equivalent roles at a major western multinational corporation.
...
- Native-level fluency in English (written and verbal) with ability to communicate legal and technical nuance to US-based leadership.
...
- Foundational understanding of copyright and trademark infringement principles; deep familiarity with common infringement reporting methods (DMCA takedowns, social media platform reporting tools, etc.).
...
- Sophisticated understanding of gaming ecosystems including virtual currencies, modding communities, and virtual asset marketplaces.
...
- An investigative mindset capable of identifying and effectively targeting the financial and technical support structures for infringing commercial operations.
...
- Proficiency with IP enforcement dashboards and/or digital evidence-gathering tools.
...

...

results.title: Senior JD Edwards Inventory Distribution Business Analyst at Take-Two Interactive - Austin, Texas | $80,000 - $115,000 | SportsTechJobs
highlights:
# Take-Two Interactive is hiring a Senior JD Edwards Inventory Distribution Business Analyst
...
Reporting directly to the Director of ERP, you will serve as the functional architect and strategic lead for our corporate JD Edwards EnterpriseOne 9.2 ecosystem. You will drive optimization, streamline 3rd-party integrations, and own the functional roadmap across Customer Service, Order Fulfillment, Procurement, Inventory, and Warehouse Operations.
...
- Hard Requirement: Minimum 7–10+ years of direct, hands-on experience working with JDE EnterpriseOne (9.2 preferred).
- What this role is NOT: This is not a general Data Engineering, Data Analytics, or Business Intelligence (BI) role. Candidates without heavy, native JDE configuration, JDE EDI (F47 tables), and JDE Distribution module experience will not be considered.
...
- Core Module Ownership: Lead design and configuration for JDE E1 Distribution (Inventory, Sales Order Management & Fulfillment). Manufacturing and Procurement are highly valued.
...
- EDI & 3PL Integration: Own the design, processing, and seamless integration of JDE EDI with 3PL partners.
...
- Modernization & Automation: Translate complex business requirements into functional specs.
...
- End-to-End Delivery: Partner closely with technical teams, internal EDI department and third-party vendors to oversee solution development, rigorous regression testing, and data integrity root-cause analysis.
...
- Tier 3 Escalation:
...
as the ultimate
...
process re-engineering.
...
- Deep JDE Expertise: 7–10+ years of hands-on experience as a JDE EnterpriseOne Consultant or Senior Business Analyst. JDE Certification is a major plus.
- Integration Fluent: Proven track record of implementing, optimizing, and scaling complex JDE environments and external interfaces.
...
- Consultative Mindset: Exceptional communication skills with the ability to challenge conventional thinking, brief leadership, and engage cross-functional teams.
...
- Education: Bachelor’s degree in IT, Business, or a related field.
...
- Advanced EDI Proficiency: Direct experience with EDI and specific transaction sets: 850, 856, 940, 943, 944, 947, 846, 180, 810 and 888.
- JDE Orchestrator: Ability to leverage Orchestrator, E1 Web Pages, UDOs to enhance workflows and reporting capabilities.
- JDE Development: Understanding of standard JDE development and the ability to read/translate existing code, NER and BSFN.
- JDE Functional: Understanding of JDE modules such as finance (DMAAI), manufacturing with respect to Inventory / Distribution as well as security setup.
...
Regular, full-time employees are also eligible for a range of benefits at the Company, including: medical, dental, vision, and basic life insurance coverage; 14 paid holidays per calendar year; paid vacation time per calendar year (ranging from 15 to 25 days) or eligibility to participate in the Company’s discretionary time off program; up to 10 paid sick days per calendar year; paid parental and compassionate leave; wellbeing programs for mental health and other wellness support; family planning support through Maven; commuter benefits; and reimbursements for fitness-related expenses. The use of Artificial Intelligence is not being used to screen candidates. The position is for an existing vacancy.

results.title: Sr Applied Scientist at 2k - Dublin, Ireland | $90,000 - $110,000 | SportsTechJobs
highlights:
Sr Applied Scientist at 2k - Dublin, Ireland | $90,000 - $110,000 | SportsTechJobs
...
# 2k is hiring a Sr Applied Scientist
...
2K is looking for a Sr. Applied Scientist to join the Applied AI Team in the Product Organization, with a focus on our flagship franchise NBA 2K. Our team leverages AI, machine learning, statistics, and operations research, and economics to build systems that power core game experiences. We develop algorithmic solutions in areas such as matchmaking, in-game experience personalization, player engagement, in-game economy, content optimization, and fraud detection.
...
What You’ll Do
...
- Design, develop, and iterate on machine learning models that directly impact in-game experiences and core systems (e.g., matchmaking, skill rating systems, recommender systems).
- Build and refine representation learning models to capture player skill, lineup/build strength, and interaction patterns from sequential gameplay data.
...
- Stay informed on state-of-the-art techniques, evaluate their applicability, and lead efforts to integrate promising methods into our systems.
...
- Formulate ambiguous product problems into well-defined machine learning tasks.
- Lead technical discussions, advocate for best practices in model development and deployment, and ensure scientific rigor.
...
- Collaborate closely with engineers, MLEs, and product stakeholders to integrate models into production systems and influence game design decisions.
...
We are seeking a senior scientist with strong foundations in machine learning and statistics, combined with hands-on experience building and applying models to real-world problems. The ideal candidate is comfortable working with imperfect data, defining problems from scratch, and applying modern modeling approaches—including deep learning and representation learning—to generate meaningful impact.
...
- Master’s or Ph.D. in a quantitative field (Mathematics, Statistics, Physics, Computer Science, Operations Research, Industrial Engineering, Electrical Engineering).
- Proven experience in applied science, machine learning, or data science roles, including experience deploying ML models in production environments.
- Proven experience with deep learning, representation learning, embedding models, or self-/semi-supervised learning.
- Fluency in Python and SQL (or similar scripting languages). Experience with big data technologies such as Apache Spark.
- Working knowledge of machine learning modeling/computation frameworks such as PyTorch or Tensorflow.
...
- Demonstrated ability to work independently, rapidly prototype solutions, and test new ideas.
...
- Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to both
...
non-technical audiences.
...
Preferred Qualifications (Helpful, but Not Required):
...
- Experience with recommendation systems, ranking systems, or user behavior modeling.
- Experience with sequence models (e.g., RNNs, Transformers), graph-based approaches, or autoencoders.
- Familiarity with cloud services (such as AWS), containerization, model orchestration, and model serving.
- Familiarity with sports analytics, sports predictive modeling, or NBA domain knowledge is a plus.
- Interest in understanding video games as products.
...
#### Salary Information
...
Salary: $90,000 - $110,000

results.title: Samarth M O
highlights:
Senior Data Platform Engineer @ Take-Two Interactive
...
Senior Data Platform Engineer at [Take-Two Interactive](https://www.linkedin.com/company/take-2-interactive-software-inc-)
...
I design, code and engineer anything Data. Passionate about engineering Data Platforms and adding value through novel solutions. So far, in my 5+ years' experience as a Data Engineer, I've had the pleasure of working on some truly challenging - and incredibly rewarding - Data & software engineering problems mostly on AWS, Databricks, Spark. results.title: Jeff Bender
highlights:
Senior Manager, People Analytics at [Take-Two Interactive](https://www.linkedin.com/company/take-2-interactive-software-inc-)
...
My experience as a data professional is defined by building something from nothing – something that endures, leaving a lasting impact. With a decade of experience across a range of industries, companies, and team dynamics, I blend comfortably into any environment and can adapt my skills to any situation or audience. If you’re looking for a data scientist to build a solid data culture at your organization – or want another helping hand to bolster the team – let me know. I’m always excited at the prospect of new questions to answer and puzzles to solve. Contact me at: Email: jeffbender09@gmail.com If you’re intrigued by the above, but want some concrete examples of my experience: - I’ve done statistical modeling for survival analysis, forecasting, clustering, and measuring impact/correlation of phenomena. I’ve built sentiment analysis models for insights on internal company surveys. - I’ve designed and led others in the execution of statistical modeling projects, also establishing standards for documentation and communication of repeatable methodology. - I’ve partnered with other internal teams to redesign or streamline data collection processes to improve data quality and consistency. - I’ve engineered data warehouses at several companies and in each case established standards for the use and interpretation of internal data. This included creating ETL flows of data between systems/tools, and creating data models for use by other developers within the company. - I’ve managed analytics tool environments and advised other teams on best practices for use of those environments. This included defining data permissions/security of highly sensitive HR data for distribution to many audiences at a large scale. - Using Tableau and Power BI, I’ve also built dashboards for visualizing insights and telling stories in data. - Using Power BI, I’ve built tools integrated with Power Apps allowing user input in visualizations for scenario modeling.

You will be encouraged to push our conception of what is possible, doing as much to advance the ambition as you will attempt to build it, ultimately bringing our richly imagined world to life.

results.title: Senior AI Data & Analytics Engineer - Goodgame Studios
highlights:
Senior AI Data & Analytics Engineer - Goodgame Studios
...
# Senior AI Data & Analytics Engineer
...
#### Support us in Agentic Analytics for a New Mobile Game!
...
We're not looking for someone who has "built a dashboard". We're looking for someone who already uses agentic workflows as a core part of how they work: Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, custom agents, MCP servers, skills, planning loops, review gates, and AI-assisted engineering. Someone with strong opinions on which AI workflows hold up in production and which fall apart when real decisions depend on the numbers.
...
You'll bring that practice to the AI-native analytics ecosystem of a brand-new mobile game. You'll define how gameplay, monetization, attribution, and player behavior data are collected, structured, and transformed into trustworthy insights, automated reporting, and AI-powered analytics tools.
...
You will work at the intersection of AI, analytics, and game development, building the data foundation of a new game from scratch. You'll define its technical direction and drive the evolution of its analytics and AI capabilities from concept to production.
...
Unlike a traditional analytics role, you'll use agents to dramatically increase your throughput while remaining the architect, reviewer, and final owner of correctness. The agents generate volume; you provide judgement.
...
###### Drive AI-Native Analytics
...
Build the semantic layer that translates raw gameplay data into validated gameplay and business concepts.
...
Develop AI-driven automated reporting, dashboards, and insight-generation workflows.
...
Evaluate and implement AI tools, agents, and workflows that improve analytics speed, reliability, and depth.
...
Review AI-generated code and analytics on critical paths before they reach production.
...
#### YOUR BACKSTORY
...
Several years of experience building analytics or data-driven systems in production environments, ideally in gaming or mobile.
...
Deep comfort with LLMs, agents, and AI-assisted engineering workflows in production environments.
...
Hands-on experience using agentic workflows and AI coding tools in real-world projects.
...
manage autonomous and multi-agent workflows, including when to intervene,
...
use MCP servers, custom tools, skills, or subagents,
...
review AI-generated code and queries without becoming a bottleneck.
...
Strong experience working with behavioral, transactional, or event-based datasets and deriving actionable insights from complex data.
...
Strong Python and SQL skills. You read and own code you did not write and verify outputs independently rather than blindly trusting generated results.
...
Experience designing scalable data architectures, pipelines, data models, dashboards, analytical frameworks, or automated insight-generation systems.
...
Strong analytical decomposition skills and the ability to translate ambiguous business questions into precise analytical problems.
...
###### NICE TO HAVE
...
Gaming or mobile analytics experience (monetization, attribution, retention, LiveOps, economy).
...
Event-driven and streaming data architectures.
...
Real-time telemetry or anti-cheat / anomaly-detection systems.
...
Published or shared work on agentic workflows – blog posts, OSS subagents/skills, internal tooling you've open-sourced.
...
Expect to walk us through an agentic workflow you've actually built and used – the specs and context you feed it, how you structure the loop, where it breaks, how you catch it, and what you'd never let an agent do unsupervised on data people make decisions from. Bring screen-shares, repos, prompts, whatever shows the real thing. We're more interested in your judgement and battle scars than in polished slides.
...

results.title: Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist) | Wfh
highlights:
Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist) | Wfh
...
# Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist)
...
Join the core team at Eclipse, where we’re building an AI agent-first marketplace that connects intelligence with real-world tasks, starting with data collection and labeling. We are seeking a Data Scientist to establish the foundation for how our data is labeled, processed, and prepared for consumption by next-generation Large Language Models (LLMs). Your work will be critical in transforming our raw data collections into valuable, AI-ready datasets.
...
- Proven experience as a Data Scientist or Machine Learning Engineer with a focus on data quality and preparation.
- Strong understanding of data labeling methodologies and hands-on experience with data annotation platforms and workflows.
...
- Demonstrated experience preparing datasets for training and fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs), including knowledge of techniques like tokenization, embeddings, and NER.
...
- Proficiency in Python and common data science libraries (e.g., Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, spaCy, Hugging Face).
...
- Experience using APIs/SDKs to automate data annotation and active learning loops.
...
- Responsibilities
...
- Develop Data Labeling Strategies: Design and document a formal data annotation strategy, including clear, scalable, and efficient guidelines for labeling our data. Define and enforce quality metrics, including inter-annotator agreement.
...
- Optimize for LLM Consumption: Research, define, and prototype the optimal data formats, structures, and pre-processing steps required for fine-tuning and training LLMs on our datasets.
...
- Data Quality Analysis: Establish automated processes and metrics to analyze the quality of both raw and labeled data, providing feedback to improve our data collection and labeling workflows.
...
- Collaborate with Engineering: Work closely with the engineering team to guide the implementation of data processing pipelines and ensure the data infrastructure meets the needs of ML applications.
...
- Nice-to-Haves
- Experience with audio data processing and relevant libraries.
- Familiarity with data annotation platforms and tools.
- Knowledge of modern MLOps principles and practices.
- Experience with large language model data curation and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) pipelines.

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.
